blurb: iOS and iPadOS Data Protection assigns every file and keychain item a protection class determining when its class key is available. Class A (Complete) keys are available only when the device is unlocked; Class B (CompleteUnlessOpen) keys remain available for files opened before lock; Class C (CompleteUntilFirstUserAuthentication) keys persist in memory after first unlock until reboot; Class D (NoProtection) keys derive from the UID alone. Each per-file key is encrypted with the applicable class key, itself wrapped with a combination of the hardware UID and user passcode-derived key, maintained in a keybag stored in Effaceable Storage.
